title: Smoky Charred Ethiopian Almond Flour Stew
description: A pillowy, charred stew that marries Ethiopian flair with a creamy almond flour base, finished in a terracotta dish for a romantic, FODMAP-friendly date night.
instructions: Preheat your terracotta dish over medium-high heat until it is very hot.
Drizzle the olive oil into the dish and swirl to coat the bottom, then add the diced carrots, zucchini, bell pepper, and eggplant, letting them sear for 3-4 minutes until charred spots appear.
Sprinkle the cumin and paprika over the vegetables, stir well, and cook for another 2 minutes to bloom the spices.
Pour in the low sodium vegetable broth, add the tomato paste, and bring the mixture to a gentle simmer, covering the dish with a lid to trap steam.
Reduce the heat to low and stir in the almond flour, whisking to dissolve and thicken the stew into a silky, pillowy consistency.
Add the diced spinach and the shio-koji, folding until the spinach wilts and the broth absorbs the fermented umami.
Season with a pinch of black pepper and a squeeze of lemon juice, tasting and adjusting the seasoning as needed.
Transfer the dish to the preheated terracotta pot, letting the surface caramelize for 2 minutes to deepen the smoky flavor.
Serve the stew hot straight from the terracotta, garnishing with freshly chopped parsley or cilantro if desired.
Pair with a side of flatbread or a small bowl of low FODMAP rice to soak up the sauce.
